text.title: Kubernetes Canary and Progressive Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Canary and Progressive Delivery`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each canary step MUST gate on at least one SLO metric (error-rate AND latency); promote-on-time-only steps are forbidden.

rationale.source: Argo Rollouts analysis docs, Flagger docs

rule.rationale: Time-only steps promote bad releases silently; SLO gates catch regressions before full traffic.

rule.statement: On SLO breach the canary MUST auto-rollback within 5 minutes; manual-only rollback is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Manual rollback is too slow during peak traffic; auto-rollback bounds blast radius.

rule.statement: Weight ramp MUST have at least 3 steps (e.g., 5 25 50 100); single-jump canaries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Single-jump canaries cannot detect threshold-class regressions early.

rule.statement: Analysis MUST use a versioned AnalysisTemplate with explicit successCondition / failureCondition; inline queries per Rollout are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Inline queries drift across services; templates make policy changes one-place.

rule.statement: Stateful workloads (databases, queues, in-memory caches) MUST NOT use canary; use shadow / blue-green / migration instead.
rule.rationale: Mixing versions across writers corrupts state.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
